author: @matthewszostek1819
text: Or combine it with your variable sponge resistor to make an analog magnetic field controlled variable  resistor

author: @matthewszostek1819
text: You could impregnate nitrile gloves with it and be able to sense magnetic fields with your hands much like bio hackers with magnet finger implants...without the scalpel and blood.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: now that's an idea - actually i like it

author: @roidroid
text:
This could make some really hilarious prank graffiti.
When they paint over it, you just sprinkle iron filings onto the wall and suddenly the graffiti is back, hahaha.
Maybe we should call it *Graphiti*.  _"Coz it's P.H. Fat, ain't it"_.

Not sure if i got the physics right, perhaps you'd have to momentarily electrify the underlying graphiti lettering, turning it into an electromagnet, before the iron filings will stick.
(you'd spray the whole wall with glue so when the iron filings are magnetically attracted - they stay).

If you paint the wall with different (insulated) layers of conductive ink, you could electrify different combinations of this circuitry and have some fine control over where the iron filings will stick at any time.  Do it as a grid, like a display.  You could even do it in batches, with each iron filing batch a different colour, to give a controlled multi-colour image.

It seems like it's turning the wall into a giant etch-a-sketch.
I wonder if you could create a giant animated display wall with this technique.

author: @davidtatum1090
text:
if you could make it change polarity when a current is run through it, you could make a large notice board, like a train station board, similar to the way e-ink works. 

for example, ping pong balls that have a hole drilled completely through them, half coated with black magnetic paint, placed on a loose axle (through the hole).  flip the polarity on the wall behind, and the ball flips from the half white, to the half black side. magnetic pixel.
